content:
# Final Review Proposal
**Producer:** agent:codex/gpt-5.4-xhigh

## Summary

Reviewed the bead `9ni.6.2` changes and ran the test suite. I found one correctness issue: planned-elsewhere context still pulls plan-derived acceptance ownership/summaries when bead membership in the current plan is unconfirmed, which can inject unrelated out-of-scope guidance into the generated prompt.

## Amendments

1. Fix the unconfirmed-membership path in `build_planned_elsewhere_context` (`src/cli/project.rs:1357-1450`). The function correctly suppresses same-workstream neighbor inference only after the later `location_hint` gate (`src/cli/project.rs:1430-1450`), but it still eagerly derives planned-elsewhere entries from `bundle.acceptance_map` and `proposal_lookup` beforehand (`src/cli/project.rs:1357-1427`). When `resolve_bead_plan` left `membership_confirmed=false` because the live bead only matched by title fallback, those plan lookups are no longer trustworthy: implicit bead IDs may now refer to a different proposal, so the generated prompt can incorrectly mark unrelated work as "already planned elsewhere" or attach the wrong plan summary. I reproduced this with a renamed live `ms-alpha.bead-2`: the prompt still included `ms-alpha.bead-4 ... shared milestone acceptance ownership in Validation (AC-1)` even though the bead/plan mapping was explicitly unconfirmed. Guard all plan-derived planned-elsewhere enrichment behind a confirmed membership check (or another proof that the referenced IDs are explicit/stable), and add a regression test for the renamed-live-bead/shared-acceptance case.
   Rationale: This is a real scope-corruption bug. In exactly the cases where the code knows the live bead cannot be matched authoritatively to the current milestone plan, it still imports plan-owned context and can tell the executing agent that unrelated work belongs elsewhere. That can suppress required implementation or cause incorrect review pushback.
